footer.tagline: The complete guide to independent Australia. Ten atlases, one map.

home.heroSubtitle: The curated guide to Australia's best independent places — every one verified, mapped, and independently run.
home.searchHelper: Search a name — or just ask, in plain English. Describe what you're after (“a gift that fits in a suitcase”) and the Atlas answers with real places.
home.heroMapAria: Open the full interactive map of Australia — every verified place, mapped
home.spectrumNavAria: Browse the ten kinds by colour
home.worthFindingIntro: A few of the independent places we think you should know about — one cover story, and a handful more.
home.learnsYourTaste: An atlas that learns your taste
home.discoverIntro: Flick through independent Australia one place at a time. Keep the ones that catch your eye — every pick quietly tunes what the Atlas shows you next.
home.roadTripIntro: You know where you're going. We'll find the stops — the makers, the lunch, the detour worth taking.
home.planAStayTitle: Tell us the kind of trip. We'll build the days.
home.planAStayIntro: Coffee to start, a long lunch in the middle, and somewhere good to stay — a day-by-day trip drawn entirely from the independent places listed across the network.
home.clusterIntro: Regions where makers, stays, culture, and food overlap. One place, many reasons to go.
home.whatsOnIntro: Festivals, markets, and long-table dinners across the network.
home.noEventsTitle: Nothing on the calendar just yet.
home.noEventsIntro: Markets, festivals, openings, and long-table dinners will appear here as they're announced.
home.newsletterIntro: New openings, the occasional essay, and the quiet finds worth a detour — one considered email a week. No noise, no algorithms.
home.kindsOfPlaceIntro: Every place we list belongs to one of {word} categories. Browse a category on its own, or search across all of them at once.
home.phOpener: Find a roaster, a winery, a gallery, a farm stay…

home.phGift: Or just ask: “a gift for my niece that's made in Australia”

trails.heroTitle: Plan a trip in plain English

trails.promptPlaceholder: Try 'weekend wine trail through the Barossa' or 'three day art tour of Hobart'...
trails.clickExample: Click any example to generate it instantly
trails.step1Desc: Tell us where, when, and what you love — wineries, makers, nature, coffee, or all of the above.
trails.step2Desc: Our engine pulls from thousands of verified listings across the Atlas network to create a day-by-day plan.
trails.step3Desc: Add stops, swap venues, save to your account, and share with friends.

trails.shareBlurb: Send the link to a friend or save it for later.
trails.buildYourOwnLine1: Plan a custom route across Australia's best independent places.

explore.picksSubtitleEmpty: When a venue vouches for another independent place, it appears here — a recommendation from the people who would know best.
explore.picksEmpty: No producer picks yet. As venues across the network vouch for one another, their picks will appear here.
explore.networkTitle: A living atlas of independent Australia
explore.networkSubtitle: Every number on this page is live. The network grows daily as venues are verified, claimed, and added across {countWord} curated directories.
explore.regionalCoverageDesc: Regions sorted by listing density. Lighter counts show where the network is still growing.
explore.missedTitle: Know a place we have missed?
explore.missedDesc: The Atlas grows best through word of mouth. If you know an independent venue, maker, or natural place that should be on the network, tell us.
explore.indepSubtitle: What it means to be on Australian Atlas.
explore.indepPledge: Every place on Australian Atlas is independently owned and operated. No chains. No franchises. No corporate groups. Just people running places they believe in.
explore.indepCriteria1Desc: The people behind the business are involved in running it day to day. Decisions are made by the people doing the work, not a board in another city.
explore.indepCriteria2Title: Not part of a national or international chain
explore.indepCriteria2Desc: No multi-site corporate brands. If it has hundreds of locations and a franchise model, it doesn’t belong here.
explore.indepCriteria3Desc: The menu, the stock, the opening hours, the character of the place — these are chosen by the operator, not dictated by a head office.
explore.indepCriteria4Desc: The best independent places are shaped by where they are. They source locally, respond to their community, and contribute to the character of a town or region.
explore.indepWhyPara1: Independent businesses are the difference between a town that feels like somewhere and one that could be anywhere. They create the texture of a place: the bakery that opens at five, the bookshop that stocks local authors, the gallery in the old post office. When you spend money at an independent business, more of it stays in the community. The owner lives nearby, hires locally, and reinvests in the street they trade on.
explore.indepWhyPara2: Australia has always been shaped by people who start things in the places they love. A cellar door in the Adelaide Hills. A ceramics studio on the mid-north coast. A coffee roaster in a Hobart laneway. These aren’t just businesses — they’re expressions of skill, taste, and commitment to a place. They make regional Australia worth the drive.
explore.indepWhyPara3: But independent places are harder to find. They don’t have the marketing budgets of chains. They don’t appear at the top of algorithm-driven platforms. Australian Atlas exists to change that — to make the independent layer of this country visible, searchable, and easy to support.
explore.indepPullQuote: The best places in Australia are the ones somebody put their name to.
explore.indepVerifyIntro: Keeping the network accurate is an ongoing process, not a one-time check. Here’s how we do it.
explore.indepVerify1Title: Every listing is reviewed before publishing
explore.indepVerify1Desc: New listings go through a verification process before they appear on the network. We check ownership structure, business registration, and chain affiliation.
explore.indepVerify2Desc: Visitors and locals can flag listings that no longer meet the criteria. If a place has been acquired by a chain or closed its doors, we hear about it quickly.
explore.indepVerify3Desc: Business owners can claim their listing for free to keep details current. Claimed listings are verified directly with the operator and updated in real time.
explore.indepVerify4Title: We remove places that no longer qualify
explore.indepVerify4Desc: If a business changes hands, joins a franchise, or no longer operates independently, we remove it from the network. No exceptions, no grandfathering.
explore.indepCtaTitle: Know an independent place that should be here?
explore.indepCtaDesc: If you know a place that’s independently owned, genuinely good, and deserves to be found — we want to hear about it.
explore.aboutHeroSubtitle: Mapping the small batch producers, the family-run stays, the bookshops, the galleries you stumble upon, and the places that make this country worth exploring slowly.
explore.aboutStory1: There is another Australia underneath the one the big platforms show you. It is the ceramics studio in a regional town, the distillery in a converted dairy, the swimming hole the locals know about, the vintage shop worth the detour. These places rarely appear on mainstream travel sites. They are too small, too independent, too particular.
explore.aboutStory2: Australian Atlas exists to map that layer. Ten curated atlases cover every dimension of independent culture across {regions} regions. Each place is checked, categorised, and maintained — a reference work, not a scrape.
explore.aboutStory2WithListings: Australian Atlas exists to map that layer. Ten curated atlases cover every dimension of independent culture across {regions} regions and {listings} verified listings. Each place is checked, categorised, and maintained — a reference work, not a scrape.
explore.aboutStory3: I started this because I kept finding places that deserved more visibility than an Instagram post or a Google pin. A distiller doing something genuinely interesting in a shed outside Castlemaine. A bookshop in a town of 400 people. These are the places that make a region worth the drive, and nobody was mapping them properly.
explore.belief1Body: No chains. No franchises. No paid placements. Every listing in the network exists because someone built something real, not because they bought visibility. If a place is here, it is because it is independently run and worth knowing about.
explore.belief2Body: Every listing is verified, geocoded, and categorised. We check URLs, phone numbers, and business status. Venues that cannot be verified are excluded. We would rather have a smaller, accurate atlas than a large, unreliable one.
explore.belief3Body: Australia has an extraordinary independent layer: the makers, the growers, the people running galleries in converted woolsheds and roasting coffee in country towns. Most platforms miss it. We exist to map it.
explore.belief4Body: Australian Atlas is built and maintained by one person in Australia. It is not a venture-backed startup chasing growth metrics. It is a reference work, built with care, and intended to last.
explore.aboutNetworkSubtitle: Each atlas covers a distinct category of independent place. Together, they form the most comprehensive guide to independent Australia.
explore.atlas_sba_desc: Craft drink. The distillers, brewers, winemakers, and cidermakers doing it independently.
explore.atlas_craft_desc: Makers. Ceramicists, woodworkers, textile artists, glassblowers, and studio potters.
explore.atlas_collection_desc: Museums, galleries, heritage spaces, and artist-run initiatives worth the visit.
explore.atlas_fine_grounds_desc: Specialty coffee. Single-origin roasters, independent cafes, and the places that take it seriously.
explore.atlas_rest_desc: Boutique stays. The cabin in the ranges, the guesthouse on the coast, the converted shed.
explore.atlas_field_desc: Natural places. Swimming holes, walking trails, lookouts, and the landscapes between towns.
explore.atlas_corner_desc: Independent shops. Bookshops, record stores, design studios, and retailers who stock with intent.
explore.atlas_found_desc: Vintage and secondhand. Op shops, antique dealers, salvage yards, and the thrill of the find.
explore.atlas_table_desc: Food producers. Farm gates, bakeries, providores, and the people growing and making what you eat.
explore.atlas_way_desc: Experiences. Guided walks, cultural tours, sailing charters, and the people who know the country.
explore.audTravellersBody: Discover independent places across the country. Build trails, save favourites, and plan trips around the things that actually make a region interesting.
explore.audOperatorsBody: If you run an independent venue, your listing may already be here. Claim it for free to update your details, or subscribe for enhanced features.
explore.audCouncilsBody: Tourism bodies and regional councils get access to verified data, regional dashboards, and embeddable content for their own platforms.
explore.aboutCommunityTitle: Know a place we should list?
explore.aboutCommunityDesc: The network gets better when people contribute. If you know an independent place that should be listed — a maker, a producer, a shop, a swimming hole — let me know.
explore.aboutPartOf: Australian Atlas is part of <link>Australian Heritage</link>, an editorial network focused on Australian culture, place, and identity.

explore.indexMetaDescription: Browse all {count} independent Australian places alphabetically. The complete A-Z directory of the Australian Atlas network.
explore.indexMetaOgDescription: Browse all {count} independent Australian places alphabetically.

developers.metaDescription: Access verified independent listing data across ten curated directories via the Australian Atlas API.
developers.heroSubhead: Read-only access to verified, curated data on independent businesses across Australia. Ten verticals, 80+ regions, updated continuously.
developers.authDescription: All requests require an API key passed via the <header>x-api-key</header> header or <param>api_key</param> query parameter.
developers.endpointVenuesDesc: List all verified venues. Filter by vertical, region, state.
developers.endpointVenueByIdDesc: Get a single venue by ID.
developers.endpointRegionsDesc: List all mapped regions. Filter by state.
developers.endpointRegionVenuesDesc: List all venues in a specific region. Filter by vertical.
developers.useCaseTourismDesc: Integrate verified independent venue data into your travel or discovery app.
developers.useCaseCouncilDesc: Pull independent business data for spatial analysis and regional planning.
developers.useCaseWritersDesc: Build custom itineraries from verified listing data across ten categories.
developers.useCaseResearchersDesc: Study independent business distribution, regional economic patterns, and cultural geography across Australia.
developers.dataPolicyPrivacy: The API does not expose PII, contact details of unclaimed venues, or internal scoring data. All data returned is already publicly visible on the Atlas Network websites.
developers.dataPolicyAttribution: Attribution is required: include {attribution} with a link to {url} in any public-facing use of the data.
developers.ctaBody: API keys are issued on request. Tell us what you are building and we will get you set up.

eventsSubmit.metaDescription: Event submissions are opening soon on Australian Atlas. Register your interest by email and we will let you know the moment they open.
eventsSubmit.heading: Event submissions are opening soon
eventsSubmit.body: We're putting the finishing touches on event listings across the Atlas network. If you run a festival, market, dinner, tour, exhibition or workshop, email us and we'll let you know the moment submissions open.
eventsSubmit.confirmationBody: We'll review it within 48 hours and let you know when it goes live. A confirmation email has been sent to your inbox.

forCouncils.metaTitle: For Regional Councils & Tourism Bodies | Australian Atlas
forCouncils.metaDescription: Partner with Australian Atlas to surface independent businesses in your region. Verified listings, editorial content, and discovery infrastructure across {count} categories.
forCouncils.bannerText: Australian Atlas for councils is in free founding beta. Founding partners get full access at no cost while the product matures.
forCouncils.heroSubtitle: Australian Atlas is a verified, editorially curated network of {listings} independent places across {count} categories and {regions} regions. Built and operated in Australia.
forCouncils.whatCard1Desc: Wineries and breweries. Galleries and heritage sites. Makers and studios. Coffee roasters. Restaurants and eateries. Boutique stays. Natural places. Independent retail. Vintage and antiques. Farm gates and food producers.
forCouncils.whatCard2Desc: Every listing is location-verified, contact-audited, and categorised across the network. No self-serve submissions without review. No paid placement.
forCouncils.whatCard3Desc: From the Barossa to Byron, Gippsland to the Goldfields. Bounding-box geographic anchoring means every listing belongs to a real place, not a marketing label.
forCouncils.whyTitle: The independent layer your existing platforms miss
forCouncils.whyPara1: Your region has an ATDW presence and a council tourism website. Those platforms cover accredited operators and major attractions. What they typically don’t cover is the independent layer: the cellar door that opened last year, the ceramics studio operating from a converted shed, the vintage shop that draws weekend visitors from three hours away.
forCouncils.whyPara2: These places are often the actual reason people visit a region. Australian Atlas maps them systematically — verified, categorised, and positioned within a national discovery network that connects makers, producers, and cultural spaces across the country.
forCouncils.whyPara3: We’re not competing with your existing tourism assets. We’re filling the gap between what those platforms capture and what your region actually offers.
forCouncils.discoveryTitle: How people find your region is changing
forCouncils.discoveryIntro: Search engine optimisation (SEO) is still essential. But a new category — generative engine optimisation (GEO) — is emerging fast. Councils that understand both will have a structural advantage.
forCouncils.seoDesc: Google ranks pages based on relevance, authority, and structure. When someone searches “best wineries Barossa Valley,” pages with verified, well-structured listing data outperform thin directory pages and generic tourism copy.
forCouncils.seoFeature2: Canonical URLs per venue, per vertical
forCouncils.geoDesc: AI tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews now answer travel questions directly. When someone asks “plan a weekend in the Adelaide Hills,” the AI assembles an answer from structured, authoritative sources it can cite.
forCouncils.geoFeature2: Entity-level data: name, location, category, description
forCouncils.geoFeature4: Verified data preferred over unstructured pages
forCouncils.earlyPara1: AI models build knowledge from the structured data they can access today. Regions with well-organised, verified, consistently cited listing data will be recommended more reliably than regions without it.
forCouncils.earlyPara2: Australian Atlas provides this infrastructure: verified entities, structured metadata, canonical URLs, and cross-linked editorial content across {count} specialist verticals. Your region’s independent businesses become discoverable in both traditional and generative search.
forCouncils.earlyPara3: The councils that move early will define how their regions are described by AI for years to come.
forCouncils.partnerCard1Desc: The verified, categorised record of every independent business operating in your region. Know exactly what’s open, where, and in which category: the cellar doors, studios, and producers that never reach ATDW or your tourism site. This is the layer most regional data misses, mapped and audited.
forCouncils.partnerCard2Desc: Your region featured editorially across the Australian Atlas network: homepage, discovery trails, regional pages, and the journal. Not an ad — a genuine editorial presence.
forCouncils.partnerCard3Desc: Curate editorial trails, regional picks, and seasonal guides for your area. Your local knowledge, published through a platform that reaches independent-minded travellers nationally.
forCouncils.partnerCard4Desc: From the day you join, your region builds a performance baseline: weekly trend lines for page views and clicks, visitor origin, search interest — including the searches that found nothing (your demand gaps) — and how your region ranks against every other Atlas region. Bot-filtered, exportable, and delivered to your inbox monthly as the Region Pulse.
forCouncils.exampleReportNote: A live sample built from real Australian Atlas data — the actual deliverable, not a mock-up.
forCouncils.betaTitle: Free right now, while we’re in beta
forCouncils.betaIntro: Australian Atlas for councils is in free founding beta. Founding partners get full access at no cost while the product matures — no card, no tiers to weigh up. One offering, everything included.
forCouncils.foundingPartnersGetIntro: You’re set up as a founding partner with full access to everything below — the complete council toolkit, with nothing held back behind a higher tier.
forCouncils.betaFeature1: Your region’s complete verified listing data, with performance (views & clicks)
forCouncils.betaFeature2: A live region dashboard, kept current automatically
forCouncils.betaFeature3: Multiple regions in a single dashboard
forCouncils.betaFeature4: Weekly trend analytics — views, clicks, visitor origin and unique visitors, with period-on-period change
forCouncils.betaFeature5: Search-demand insights — what visitors looked for, and the demand gaps no spend data can see
forCouncils.betaFeature6: A digital presence audit of your local operators, with exportable hit-lists for capability programs
forCouncils.betaFeature7: Benchmarking against every other published Atlas region
forCouncils.betaFeature8: The monthly Region Pulse — your region’s numbers in your inbox on the 1st
forCouncils.betaFeature9: Regional content co-creation — trails, editorials, picks and seasonal guides
forCouncils.betaFeature10: An embeddable live region map for your own website
forCouncils.betaFeature11: A councillor-ready white-label report (your branding) to share or print
forCouncils.betaFeature12: Export your region’s full listing data (CSV)
forCouncils.betaFeature13: A direct line to the team for support and feedback
forCouncils.signInLink: Sign in to your council dashboard
forCouncils.faq1Q: How does this sit alongside ATDW?
forCouncils.faq1A: ATDW focuses on operator-submitted tourism listings. Australian Atlas covers the independent layer that those platforms typically miss: the small-batch winery that doesn’t list on ATDW, the maker studio without a tourism accreditation, the vintage shop that’s a genuine draw but isn’t on any register. We’re complementary infrastructure, not a replacement.
forCouncils.faq2A: Every listing in our network is verified against its source data: confirmed active, correct location, validated contact details. We run automated audits across the full database and flag anything that fails. Venues marked as AI-generated carry a disclaimer and are excluded from editorial content until human-verified.
forCouncils.faq3A: None. While we’re in beta it’s free for founding partners, with full access and no lock-in. You can step away at any time, and we’ll give partners plenty of notice before anything changes.
forCouncils.faq4A: We have verified listings across every state and territory, with the deepest coverage in Victoria, New South Wales, South Australia, and Tasmania. Regional editorial content is growing, and council partnerships directly accelerate coverage in specific areas.
forCouncils.faq5Q: Can we contribute our own data?
forCouncils.faq5A: Yes. Founding partners can suggest operators for consideration, with operators opting in themselves, flag corrections, and co-create editorial content for their region. All submissions go through our verification pipeline before publishing.
forCouncils.faq6A: Australian Atlas is independently operated and Australian-owned. It’s part of the Australian Heritage editorial network. The platform is built and maintained by a small team focused on documenting independent Australia.
forCouncils.contactPara1: I’m Matt, the founder of Australian Atlas. If you represent a council, a tourism body, or a regional organisation and want to talk about what a partnership could look like for your area, I’d like to hear from you.
forCouncils.contactPara2: Not a sales pitch — a genuine conversation about your region and how we might be useful.

forTrade.metaTitle: Atlas Trade — for tour operators, DMCs & trip designers | Australian Atlas
forTrade.metaDescription: A pre-vetted set of independent Australian operators and an attributed itinerary builder, for the travel trade. Free founding beta.
forTrade.betaBannerText: Atlas Trade is in free founding beta. A capped founding cohort gets full access at no cost while the product matures.
forTrade.heroTitleLine1: A vetted set of independent operators,
forTrade.heroTitleLine2: and a way to build with it
forTrade.heroSubhead: Atlas Trade gives tour operators, DMCs, and trip designers a pre-vetted network of {count} independent Australian places — and a plain-language builder to assemble attributed itineraries from it.
forTrade.cardNetworkDesc: {listings} independent operators across {categories} categories and {regions} regions — location-verified, contact-audited, no paid placement. Every stop in every itinerary links to a live, verified record: nothing here can be an AI's invention.
forTrade.cardToolkitDesc: A filterable product directory (region, state, group size, coach access, trade terms), one-page trade fact sheets with a PDF for every trade-ready venue, and tracked rates, availability and famil enquiries — the contracting groundwork, minus the phone tag.
forTrade.cardProposalsDesc: Describe the tour in plain language, assemble day-structured itineraries with drive estimates, add a cover note, and share under "Prepared by you · Curated via Atlas" — on a private link and a PDF. Drafts auto-save.
forTrade.independenceTitle: The independent layer is the reason people travel
forTrade.independencePara1: The cellar door that opened last year, the ceramicist working from a converted dairy, the roaster a town will drive an hour for — these are what an experienced traveller remembers, and what a good itinerary is built around. They are also the hardest to find reliably, because they rarely sit on the accredited registers.
forTrade.independencePara2: Atlas maps that layer systematically — verified, categorised, and placed within a real geography rather than a marketing label. For the trade, that is the value: a curated, defensible set you can assemble from quickly and stand behind to an international client, without vouching for places you have not checked yourself.
forTrade.independencePara3: Atlas stays non-transactional. You book direct with each operator. Where an operator has told us they welcome the trade, the builder surfaces that — trade rates, group ceilings, a note to contact them first — so the people who want your business are easy to reach, and the rest are simply good listings.
forTrade.builderTitle: From a sentence to a shareable itinerary
forTrade.step1Desc: Describe the tour in plain language, or filter the directory the way a product manager works.
forTrade.step2Desc: Capacity, notice, seasonality, insurance, languages, and a trade contact — one page, one PDF.
forTrade.step3Desc: Day-structured stops with time hints, private notes and drive estimates. Drafts auto-save.
forTrade.step4Desc: A co-branded link and PDF for the client; tracked rate and famil enquiries to the venues.
forTrade.foundingBetaSubhead: The founding cohort is capped. Members get full access to the builder at no cost during beta, lock in a founding rate at signup, and would see their first invoice aligned to the financial year on 1 July. No card, no commitment — accepting the terms is the only step.
forTrade.benefitPdfExport: PDF export carrying the Atlas attribution
forTrade.benefitTradeSignals: Trade-welcome signals where operators have opted in
forTrade.benefitFoundingRate: A founding rate locked at signup
forTrade.joinGateNote: You'll sign in, tell us who you are, and accept the terms. That's the gate.
forTrade.faqWhatIsA: A working tool, not a directory. You describe the kind of tour you are building in plain language, Atlas surfaces relevant independent operators from across its vetted network, and you assemble an ordered, attributed itinerary you can share or export. It is the same curated network behind the consumer Atlas — pointed at the people who build trips for a living.
forTrade.faqOperatorsQ: Where do the operators come from?
forTrade.faqOperatorsA: The full Australian Atlas network: small-batch wineries and distillers, makers and studios, roasters, boutique stays, natural places, farm gates. Every listing is location-verified and contact-audited. The value to you is a defensible, pre-vetted set you can stand behind to a client — the independent layer the large platforms miss.
forTrade.faqBookableQ: Is every operator bookable through Atlas?
forTrade.faqBookableA: No, and that is deliberate. Atlas is non-transactional. Some operators have told us they welcome trade enquiries and offer trade rates — where that is the case, the builder surfaces it so you can contact them directly. The rest show standard listing information. You always book direct with the operator.
forTrade.faqWhiteLabelA: No. Itineraries carry a quiet "Curated via Atlas" line — it is a condition of use, not removable. You are welcome to present them to clients under your own trip; the attribution simply stays on the artefact.
forTrade.faqCostA: Nothing during the founding beta. Founding-cohort members lock in a founding rate at signup, with the first invoice aligned to the new financial year on 1 July — there is no charge while we are in beta, and you can step away at any time.
forTrade.faqWhoRunsA: Australian Atlas is independently operated and Australian-owned, part of the Australian Heritage editorial network. It is built and maintained by a small team focused on documenting independent Australia.
forTrade.contactHeading: Talk to us first if you'd rather
forTrade.contactBody: I'm Matt, the founder of Australian Atlas. If you build trips for a living and want to understand whether the network fits the way you work, I'd like to hear from you.

press.metaDescription: Live network stats, vertical overviews, and media contact information for the Australian Atlas network — ten curated directories mapping independent Australia.
press.liveStatsNote: Stats on this page are live from the Australian Atlas database.
press.networkOverviewPara1: Australian Atlas is a network of ten curated directories mapping independent Australia — the breweries, galleries, bookshops, vintage stores, farm gates, boutique stays, specialty roasters, makers, and natural places that make regions worth visiting.
press.networkOverviewPara2: Every listing is verified. No chains. No aggregator padding. The network exists to surface the kind of places that word-of-mouth built.
press.factCoverageValue: All states and territories across Australia
press.factRegionsValue: {count, plural, one {# mapped region} other {# mapped regions}}
press.factEditorialValue: Independently verified, no paid placement in editorial content
press.factTrailValue: AI-powered itinerary builder across all ten verticals
press.verticalSbaDesc: Craft breweries, wineries, distilleries, cideries and cellar doors
press.verticalCollectionDesc: Museums, galleries, heritage sites and cultural centres
press.verticalCraftDesc: Makers, artists and studios across every discipline
press.verticalFineGroundsDesc: Specialty coffee roasters and independent cafes
press.verticalRestDesc: Boutique hotels, farm stays, glamping and cottages
press.verticalFieldDesc: Swimming holes, waterfalls, lookouts and natural places
press.verticalCornerDesc: Bookshops, record stores, homewares and indie retail
press.verticalFoundDesc: Vintage stores, op shops, antique dealers and markets
press.verticalTableDesc: Farm gates, bakeries, food producers and providores
press.verticalWayDesc: Guided walks, cultural tours, sailing charters and expeditions
press.contactBody: For media enquiries, partnership discussions, or interview requests:

pricing.metaDescription: Regional intelligence, verified listing data, and editorial tools for tourism bodies and regional councils across Australia.
pricing.heroTitle: The independent layer your region data is missing
pricing.heroLead: ATDW lists the hotels and the chain restaurants. Your council tourism site lists the attractions with the biggest marketing budgets. Neither maps the independent businesses that actually make your region worth visiting — the family-run cellar door, the ceramicist who moved from Melbourne, the vintage store everyone drives forty minutes for.
pricing.heroStatsLine: Australian Atlas does. {listings}+ verified independent listings, across ten curated directories, covering {regions} regions. Every listing verified. No chains. No aggregator padding.
pricing.statsCaption: Numbers pulled live from the Australian Atlas database
pricing.getHeading: Regional intelligence, not just a listing service
pricing.benefitDataAccessDesc: Every verified independent listing in your region, across all ten atlases. Filterable, exportable, always current. See what visitors are actually searching for in your area.
pricing.benefitContentCoCreationDesc: Commission editorial trails, regional profiles, and producer features through the Atlas Journal. Content that reads like independent journalism, not destination marketing.
pricing.benefitCoverageGapDesc: See where your region is well-mapped and where listings are thin. Identify which verticals have room to grow and which independent businesses are missing from the network.
pricing.benefitDemandDataDesc: Understand what visitors are actually looking for in your region — search queries, trail generation prompts, and zero-result searches that reveal unmet demand.
pricing.benefitNetworkReachDesc: Your region appears on ten separate, SEO-optimised vertical sites plus the master portal. Cross-vertical trail generation drives multi-day visitation by combining venues across categories.
pricing.benefitQualityManagementDesc: Flag outdated information, suggest new listings, and request Journal coverage for venues in your region. Listings you champion get prioritised for verification.
pricing.networkHeading: Ten curated directories, one verified data layer
pricing.verticalWhat_found: Vintage stores, op shops, antique dealers
pricing.plansIntro: Partnerships start with a conversation about your region and what you need. Plans run annually and are scoped to the number of regions and the level of data access and editorial support you need.
pricing.planExplorerDesc: Regional data access, listing counts by vertical, basic coverage report, embeddable map widget.
pricing.planPartnerDesc: Full analytics dashboard, search demand data, content co-creation, coverage gap analysis, editorial trail commissioning.
pricing.planEnterpriseDesc: Multiple regions, API access, white-label reports, custom exports, dedicated account management.
pricing.ctaBody: Every council partnership begins with understanding what already exists in your region on the network. We can show you your listing data before you commit to anything.

profile.tagline: Your personal record of independent Australia.
profile.emptyState: Your passport is empty. Start exploring to build your record.
profile.privacyNote: Your passport is private. No public profiles, no social features. Your data can be exported or deleted at any time.

reportInfringement.metaTitle: Report a copyright or IP issue | Australian Atlas
reportInfringement.metaDescription: Notify Australian Atlas of content that infringes your copyright or other rights.
reportInfringement.heading: Report a copyright or IP issue
reportInfringement.intro: If you believe content on {brand} infringes your copyright or other rights, tell us below. We review every report and will remove infringing content where appropriate. This is an interim process and does not constitute legal advice.
reportInfringement.placeholderRightsBasis: e.g. I am the copyright owner / exclusive licensee / authorised agent
reportInfringement.labelInfringingUrl: Link to the allegedly infringing content
reportInfringement.placeholderDescription: What is being infringed, and how?
reportInfringement.goodFaithStatement: I have a good-faith belief that the use of the material described is not authorised by the rights holder, its agent, or the law, and the information in this report is accurate.
reportInfringement.successBody: Thank you. We've recorded your report and our team will review it promptly. We may contact you at the email you provided.
reportInfringement.errorRequiredFields: Please complete the required fields and confirm the good-faith statement.
reportInfringement.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
reportInfringement.errorNetwork: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.

trailsBuilder.draftRestored: Picked up where you left off.
trailsBuilder.savedPrivateNote: Saved privately to your account. Switch visibility to “Link only” and save again if you want a shareable link.

trailsBuilder.hintStops: Add at least 2 stops to save
